title: becium grandiflorum subsp. turkanaense
intro: Becium grandiflorum subsp. turkanaense is a plant species that belongs to the Lamiaceae family, commonly known as the wild African basil or East African basil. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Becium grandiflorum subsp. turkanaense include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are often employed in traditional medicine to harness its therapeutic benefits.
